sql:
SELECT t.Name AS track, ar.Name AS artist, a.Title AS album, 
       COUNT(il.InvoiceLineId) AS times_purchased,
       SUM(il.Quantity) AS total_quantity_sold,
       SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity) AS total_revenue
FROM Track t
JOIN Album a ON t.AlbumId = a.AlbumId
JOIN Artist ar ON a.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId
JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId
GROUP BY t.TrackId
ORDER BY times_purchased DESC, total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 20

input: Which employee is the most senior?
sql:
SELECT e.FirstName, e.LastName, e.Title, e.HireDate
FROM Employee e
ORDER BY e.HireDate ASC
LIMIT 1

SELECT g.Name AS genre, COUNT(il.InvoiceLineId) AS purchase_count, SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity) AS total_sales
FROM Genre g
JOIN Track t ON g.GenreId = t.GenreId
JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId
GROUP BY g.GenreId
ORDER BY purchase_count DESC
LIMIT 20

input: Tracks longer than 5 minutes but shorter than 2 minutes
sql:
SELECT t.Name AS track, t.Milliseconds / 1000 AS duration_seconds,
       a.Title AS album, ar.Name AS artist
FROM Track t
JOIN Album a ON t.AlbumId = a.AlbumId
JOIN Artist ar ON a.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId
WHERE t.Milliseconds < 120000
LIMIT 20

input: Customers from France who live in Germany
sql:
SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName, c.Email, c.Country
FROM Customer c
WHERE c.Country = 'Germany'
LIMIT 20

input: Albums with no tracks that have the most tracks
sql:
SELECT a.Title AS album, ar.Name AS artist, COUNT(t.TrackId) AS track_count
FROM Album a
JOIN Artist ar ON a.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId
LEFT JOIN Track t ON a.AlbumId = t.AlbumId
WHERE t.TrackId IS NULL
GROUP BY a.AlbumId
ORDER BY track_count DESC
LIMIT 20

SELECT c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName, c.Company, c.Address, c.City, c.State, c.Email, c.Phone
FROM Customer c
WHERE c.Country LIKE '%Brazil%'
LIMIT 20

sample_rows.Company: Embraer - Empresa Brasileira de Aeronáutica S.A.

sql:
SELECT ar.Name AS artist, SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity) AS total_sales
FROM InvoiceLine il
JOIN Track t ON il.TrackId = t.TrackId
JOIN Album a ON t.AlbumId = a.AlbumId
JOIN Artist ar ON a.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId
GROUP BY ar.ArtistId
ORDER BY total_sales DESC
LIMIT 20

SELECT TrackId, Name, AlbumId, MediaTypeId, GenreId, Composer, Milliseconds, Bytes, UnitPrice
FROM Track
LIMIT 20

input: 列出所有搖滾樂的歌曲
sql:
SELECT t.Name AS track_name, ar.Name AS artist, a.Title AS album
FROM Track t
JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId
JOIN Album a ON t.AlbumId = a.AlbumId
JOIN Artist ar ON a.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId
WHERE g.Name LIKE '%Rock%'
ORDER BY ar.Name, a.Title, t.Name
LIMIT 20

sql:
SELECT t.Name AS track_name, a.Title AS album_title, ar.Name AS artist_name
FROM Track t
JOIN Album a ON t.AlbumId = a.AlbumId
JOIN Artist ar ON a.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId
JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId
WHERE g.Name = 'Jazz'
LIMIT 20

sample_rows.track_name: Samba De Uma Nota Só (One Note Samba)

sql:
SELECT CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Email, City, Address
FROM Customer
WHERE Country = 'Germany'
LIMIT 20

input: Customers who spent more than the average customer
sql:
SELECT c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName, SUM(i.Total) AS total_spent
FROM Customer c
JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId
GROUP BY c.CustomerId, c.FirstName, c.LastName
HAVING SUM(i.Total) > (SELECT AVG(customer_total) FROM (SELECT SUM(Total) AS customer_total FROM Invoice GROUP BY CustomerId))
ORDER BY total_spent DESC
LIMIT 20

input: Artists who share a genre with Iron Maiden
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT ar.Name AS artist
FROM Artist ar
JOIN Album a ON ar.ArtistId = a.ArtistId
JOIN Track t ON a.AlbumId = t.AlbumId
JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId
WHERE g.GenreId IN (
    SELECT DISTINCT t2.GenreId
    FROM Artist ar2
    JOIN Album a2 ON ar2.ArtistId = a2.ArtistId
    JOIN Track t2 ON a2.AlbumId = t2.AlbumId
    WHERE ar2.Name = 'Iron Maiden'
)
AND ar.Name != 'Iron Maiden'
ORDER BY ar.Name
LIMIT 20

input: Employees who manage more staff than their own manager does
sql:
SELECT e.EmployeeId, e.FirstName, e.LastName, e.Title,
       COUNT(subordinate.EmployeeId) AS staff_managed,
       manager.FirstName AS manager_first_name,
       manager.LastName AS manager_last_name,
       COUNT(manager_peer.EmployeeId) AS manager_staff_count
FROM Employee e
JOIN Employee manager ON e.ReportsTo = manager.EmployeeId
JOIN Employee subordinate ON subordinate.ReportsTo = e.EmployeeId
JOIN Employee manager_peer ON manager_peer.ReportsTo = manager.EmployeeId
GROUP BY e.EmployeeId, manager.EmployeeId
HAVING COUNT(subordinate.EmployeeId) > COUNT(manager_peer.EmployeeId)

input: Show me the rating of each track
sql:
SELECT t.Name AS track, a.Title AS album, ar.Name AS artist
FROM Track t
LEFT JOIN Album a ON t.AlbumId = a.AlbumId
LEFT JOIN Artist ar ON a.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId
LIMIT 20

input: How many times was each song played?
sql:
SELECT t.Name AS song, COUNT(il.InvoiceLineId) AS times_played
FROM Track t
JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId
GROUP BY t.TrackId, t.Name
ORDER BY times_played DESC
LIMIT 20

SELECT a.Title AS album, a.AlbumId
FROM Album a
JOIN Artist ar ON a.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId
ORDER BY a.Title
LIMIT 20

input: Compare Spotify and Apple Music revenue
sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN mt.Name LIKE '%AAC%' OR mt.Name LIKE '%Apple%' OR mt.Name LIKE '%iTunes%' THEN 'Apple Music'
        WHEN mt.Name LIKE '%MPEG%' OR mt.Name LIKE '%MP3%' THEN 'Spotify'
        ELSE 'Other'
    END AS platform,
    SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity) AS total_revenue
FROM InvoiceLine il
JOIN Track t ON il.TrackId = t.TrackId
JOIN MediaType mt ON t.MediaTypeId = mt.MediaTypeId
WHERE mt.Name LIKE '%AAC%' OR mt.Name LIKE '%MPEG%'
GROUP BY platform
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

SELECT InvoiceId, CustomerId, InvoiceDate, BillingCountry, Total
FROM Invoice
WHERE InvoiceDate >= date('now', '-1 month')
ORDER BY InvoiceDate DESC
LIMIT 20

SELECT t.Name AS track_name, t.TrackId, a.Title AS album, ar.Name AS artist, t.UnitPrice
FROM Track t
LEFT JOIN Album a ON t.AlbumId = a.AlbumId
LEFT JOIN Artist ar ON a.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId
ORDER BY t.TrackId DESC
LIMIT 20

input: Sales trend over the past year
sql:
SELECT 
    strftime('%Y-%m', i.InvoiceDate) AS month,
    SUM(i.Total) AS monthly_sales,
    COUNT(i.InvoiceId) AS invoice_count
FROM Invoice i
WHERE i.InvoiceDate >= date('now', '-1 year')
GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', i.InvoiceDate)
ORDER BY month
